meta.title: Critical Minerals — Long-Term Hold Research
meta.description: Scored research universe for long-term holders across supply-constrained critical minerals.
meta.disclaimer: Research/educational only; not investment advice. Scores are subjective judgments, not price targets. Figures approximate as of June 2026; verify before acting.

factors.label: Criticality
factors.desc: Strategic importance & supply scarcity of the mineral.

factors.label: Geo leverage
factors.desc: US import reliance & China/Russia supply concentration.

factors.label: Balance sheet
factors.desc: Liquidity vs. burn and financing risk.

factors.label: Valuation/upside
factors.desc: Torque: market cap vs. resource/NPV and re-rating room.

factors.label: Theme purity
factors.desc: How directly the fund targets critical minerals.

entities.name: MP Materials

thesis.summary: Only US mine-to-magnet integrated REE producer; government price floor + Apple offtake.
thesis.detail: The cornerstone US rare-earth holding. The only fully integrated mine-to-magnet producer in the Western Hemisphere (Mountain Pass, CA + Fort Worth magnets). The Pentagon is the largest shareholder (~15%, $400M) with a 10-yr NdPr price floor (~$110/kg), a $150M loan, and a $500M Apple magnet offtake ($200M prepaid). Record NdPr output and revenue doubling YoY. Trades at a very high multiple and is still loss-making, so the strategic floor — not the current P&L — is the thesis. Catalysts: magnet ramp into the Jan-2027 DFARS defense deadline, 10X expansion. Risk: extreme valuation, NdPr price swings.

catalysts.description: Magnet ramp into DFARS defense-sourcing deadline

invalidation_triggers: China permanently lifts rare-earth export curbs and NdPr prices collapse below the DoD floor
Loss of the DoD price floor / government support

entities.name: Energy Fuels
entities.govt_support: DPA Title III eligible; AUD80M (Donald); DoW Office of Strategic Capital $725M conditional loan commitment (signed Jun 18 2026) for RE metals/alloy facility + White Mesa metallization buildout

thesis.summary: Largest US uranium producer AND only US heavy-REE separation; fortress balance sheet.
thesis.detail: A rare dual critical-minerals play: America's largest uranium producer AND the only US facility separating heavy rare earths (first commercial Dy and Tb oxides in 2026). Fortress balance sheet (~$910M, ~no debt). White Mesa Mill (UT) is the hub; targeting some of the lowest-cost NdPr in the world (~$29/kg vs. the ~$110 floor). World-class Toliara feedstock (Madagascar). Catalysts: REE commercial circuit (~2027), Phase-2 expansion, uranium price. Risk: REE not yet commercial; Madagascar jurisdiction; multi-project execution.

catalysts.description: White Mesa Phase 2 REE expansion FID
DoW OSC $725M conditional loan → domestic RE metals/alloy (metallization) facility

invalidation_triggers: REE separation fails to reach commercial economics
Uranium price falls sharply and erodes cash generation

sources.title: Dept. of War Office of Strategic Capital signs $725M conditional loan commitment with Energy Fuels

thesis.summary: Largest, lowest-cost lithium producer; max operating leverage to a price recovery.
thesis.detail: The blue-chip lithium recovery play. World's largest, lowest-cost producer — maximum operating leverage to a price rebound, as Q1 2026 showed (EPS swung from a 2025 loss to a strong beat as carbonate roughly doubled). ~$2.7B liquidity, paid down $1.3B debt, pays a dividend; a $90M DoD award supports the Kings Mountain (NC) restart. Catalysts: sustained lithium-price recovery, Kings Mountain permitting, cost cuts. Risk: highly cyclical/price-sensitive; large-cap, so less multibagger torque than the juniors.

thesis.summary: Low-cost producing brine ramping into a rising price deck; Ganfeng JV.
Producing low-cost spodumene with explosive margins; capacity doubling into 2027.
Newly merged Piedmont/Sayona hard-rock platform; path to 315kt/yr.
Lowest-cost brine at scale; Codelco JV secures Atacama to 2060; pays dividend.

entities.name: Cameco

thesis.summary: Blue-chip full-fuel-cycle play + 49% of Westinghouse; lowest-risk nuclear exposure.
thesis.detail: The lowest-risk way to own the nuclear super-cycle. Largest Western uranium producer (McArthur River, Cigar Lake) plus 49% of Westinghouse (AP1000 reactor OEM) and a stake in laser enrichment. Q1 uranium EBITDA up sharply; ~$2.6B India supply deal. The $80B+ Westinghouse/US-government AP1000 build-out partnership underpins decades of demand. Catalysts: AP1000 orders, contract pricing, AI-driven demand. Risk: premium valuation; Inkai (Kazakhstan) geopolitics.

invalidation_triggers: Uranium price reverses durably below incentive levels

entities.name: Uranium Energy

thesis.summary: Debt-free, unhedged US ISR producer scaling into a tightening market.
thesis.detail: Debt-free, unhedged US ISR producer scaling fast. ~$488M cash, ~no debt, ~1.5M lbs inventory; production commenced at Burke Hollow (TX) with two of three US ISR hubs running. Pure leverage to a rising uranium price with no hedges capping the upside. Trades at a steep multiple on current revenue (~$67M) — priced for the production ramp. Catalysts: hub ramp, inventory monetization, new offtakes. Risk: valuation vs. current revenue; if uranium stalls the growth multiple compresses.

invalidation_triggers: Uranium price stalls and the growth multiple compresses

thesis.summary: Active US ISR producer just swung to profitability with multiple Texas plants.
Low-cost US ISR producer with Shirley Basin doubling output from 2026.

entities.name: Centrus Energy

thesis.summary: The pure-play US enrichment monopoly federally funded to replace Russian supply.
thesis.detail: The strategic choke-point play. The only US-owned, US-technology enrichment company (Piketon, OH), making both LEU and HALEU — the fuel advanced reactors need and that Russia has dominated. A ~$900M DOE task order (plus up to ~$170M more) funds commercial HALEU; first significant US HALEU already delivered. The Russian-enrichment ban is a structural tailwind. Catalysts: more DOE/utility contracts, expansion financing, advanced-reactor offtakes. Risk: extreme volatility; appropriations-dependent; capacity not online until ~2029.

catalysts.description: Commercial HALEU capacity online at Piketon

invalidation_triggers: Russian enrichment returns to the US market

thesis.summary: Largest US-listed copper play leveraged to record prices + friend-shoring.
thesis.detail: The safest large-cap critical-minerals anchor. Largest US-listed copper producer with domestic smelting/refining and the leaching initiative targeting ~800M lbs/yr by 2030. Copper joined the 2025 USGS critical list; structural demand from electrification, grids and AI data centers underpins the deck (near record prices). $25.9B revenue, strong balance sheet, pays a dividend. Catalysts: US copper-tariff decision, Grasberg recovery, US expansions. Risk: Grasberg (Indonesia) disruption/guidance cut; copper cyclicality.

thesis.summary: Lowest-cost major with long reserve life; high copper-price leverage; dividend.
High-growth copper producer with Tucuma ramp adding volume into a strong deck.
US-based low-capital SX-EW copper cathode (Florence) ramping into record prices.
Diversified low-cost copper producer with US Copper World option de-risked via Mitsubishi.

entities.name: US Antimony

thesis.summary: Only US-domiciled antimony producer with a $248M federal stockpile contract.
thesis.detail: The only US-domiciled antimony producer at a moment of acute defense scarcity. A $248M sole-source DLA stockpile contract anchors revenue (2026 guidance ~$125M, up ~218%); ~$90M cash, ~debt-free. Restarting Montana mining and commissioning a new 9-furnace smelter (+300% capacity/furnace). Catalysts: smelter/mill commissioning, DLA delivery cadence. Risk: Q1 swung to a loss on ramp costs; rich on sales; antimony could soften if China's export-curb suspension (through Nov 2026) normalizes supply.

catalysts.description: DLA stockpile delivery cadence toward $125M guidance

invalidation_triggers: China's export-curb suspension normalizes antimony supply and prices

entities.name: Almonty Industries

thesis.summary: Only Western-aligned listed tungsten producer ramping into a 2027 US defense import ban.
thesis.detail: The only Western-aligned, US-listed producing tungsten company. Sangdong (South Korea) — one of the largest, highest-grade deposits — began mining late 2025 and formally commissioned March 2026; revenue +221% YoY in Q1 with positive EBITDA. Acquired a Montana project and is relocating HQ to the US, with a binding offtake to supply tungsten oxide solely for US defense ahead of a 2027 ban on Chinese tungsten in defense. ~$260M cash. Catalysts: Sangdong ramp (revenue projected to surge), Montana, EXIM financing. Risk: ramp execution; convertible-debenture volatility.

catalysts.description: US defense tungsten import ban takes effect

invalidation_triggers: Tungsten price normalizes if China curbs ease

thesis.summary: Integrated US recycler + primary lithium that won back its $115M DOE grant.
thesis.detail: An integrated US recycler that's also a primary lithium developer (Tonopah Flats, NV). Recycling revenue is ramping with a first positive gross margin, and the company just won back its $115M DOE grant on appeal (plus a separate $144M DOE award). Real, growing revenue distinguishes it from pre-revenue peers. Catalysts: recycling margin/throughput, Tonopah permitting/financing, deployment of DOE grants. Risk: still loss-making and capital-hungry; lithium-price-sensitive; volatile.

thesis.summary: OEM-qualified non-China NdFeB magnet maker scaling into the DFARS 2027 deadline.

price_targets.basis: thin coverage (single analyst $30 target, far above price)

entities.name: USA Rare Earth

thesis.summary: Cash-rich, government-backed mine-to-magnet challenger; Stillwater magnets now shipping.
thesis.detail: The well-funded challenger to MP. ~$1.75B cash after a $1.5B PIPE; Commerce Dept. backing of up to $277M plus $1.3B in CHIPS-Act loan capacity. Stillwater (OK) magnet line was commissioned March 2026 and began commercial NdFeB shipments in Q2 — so it's no longer pre-revenue on magnets. Controls the heavy-REE Round Top (TX) deposit; consolidating it via a $73M takeout of TMRC and pursuing the ~$2.8B Serra Verde deal. Catalysts: Stillwater ramp, Round Top feasibility, acquisitions. Risk: heavy cash burn and dilution; tiny revenue vs. ~$5.6B cap.

catalysts.description: Stillwater Phase 1a ramp to run-rate (600 t/yr)
Close TMRC takeout; advance Round Top feasibility

invalidation_triggers: Cash burn outruns funding and dilution accelerates
Stillwater ramp or Round Top feasibility stalls

price_targets.source: stockanalysis.com / S&P Global aggregate (~8 analysts, avg ~$37.4)

thesis.summary: Largest US lithium resource with DOE + GM backstops; ramps into a 2028 deficit.
thesis.detail: The transformational US lithium build. Thacker Pass (NV) is the largest known US lithium resource; financed by a $2.26B DOE loan with GM owning 38% of the JV (and 100% Phase-1 offtake for 20 yrs). ~$1.21B cash; mechanical completion targeted late 2027 into a forecast 2028+ deficit. The DOE + GM backstops materially de-risk financing. Catalysts: construction milestones, DOE draws, lithium price. Risk: pre-revenue execution through 2027; warrant/ATM dilution.

thesis.summary: Only US antimony source able to meet defense demand; multi-billion EXIM backing + gold.
thesis.detail: The only US mine that can meet defense antimony demand — funded. A ~$2.9B EXIM loan (approved May 2026) plus ~$59M DPA Title III backs the Stibnite (ID) build; broke ground 2025, ~450k oz/yr gold (first 4 yrs) plus antimony from 2029. Gold revenue diversifies the antimony thesis. ~$714M cash. Catalysts: EXIM definitive financing, construction milestones, gold price. Risk: large multi-year build, cost-overrun/timeline risk, financing close.

entities.name: NioCorp Developments

thesis.summary: Only US source of niobium + scandium; ~$800M EXIM close would de-risk sharply.
thesis.detail: A US-only source of niobium and scandium (both near-100% import-reliant, defense-critical) with rare-earth optionality at Elk Creek (NE). ~$307M cash; began portal excavation in 2026. The swing factor is an EXIM Letter of Interest for up to ~$800M — a close would de-risk the whole project. Highest 'upside' score in the screen on the gap between a ~$0.66B cap and the project scale. Catalysts: EXIM term sheet → commitment, updated feasibility (possibly adding REE), FID. Risk: entirely financing-dependent; large capex vs. small cap implies major dilution.

invalidation_triggers: EXIM financing fails to close (no FID)
Dilution from large capex vs. small market cap

thesis.summary: Potentially disruptive RapidSX separation tech with DoD funding; 2026-27 production runway.

entities.name: Graphite One

thesis.summary: Largest US graphite deposit + most ambitious financing pipeline; Sept 2026 permit decision.
thesis.detail: The most ambitious all-domestic graphite story. Controls Graphite Creek (AK), the largest US graphite resource, plus a planned Ohio anode + recycling complex — a full mine-to-anode-to-recycling chain. ~$37.5M DoD grant and ~$2.07B in EXIM Letters of Interest (non-binding). A FAST-41 project with a federal permit decision targeted ~Sept 2026. Top-tier upside score; clear catalyst path. Risk: very early stage, all LOIs non-binding, multi-billion financing + Alaska capex unproven; OTC liquidity.

catalysts.description: Convert EXIM letters of interest to binding commitments

invalidation_triggers: EXIM LOIs fail to convert to binding financing
Graphite price weakness undermines anode economics

price_targets.basis: OTC listing — no published US analyst targets

thesis.summary: $106M cap vs $539M-NPV permitted project with plant in hand; multibagger if it commissions.

price_targets.basis: thin coverage (2-3 analysts; targets appear stale vs price)

thesis.summary: One of few Western battery-grade manganese plays with a US grant + GM/Stellantis offtake.

price_targets.basis: OTC listing — no published US analyst targets

thesis.summary: Leading AI-power SMR with a huge data-center pipeline + fortress cash; first reactor near.
thesis.detail: The marquee 'AI-power' SMR bet. Aurora fast-reactor developer with a ~14 GW data-center pipeline (Switch up to 12 GW, Meta 1.2 GW, Equinix ~500 MW) and a fortress ~$2.5B cash. Deep DOE/Idaho National Lab ties; broke ground at INL in 2025 with first power targeted ~2027-28. Pre-revenue, so the ~$10B cap is pure optionality. Catalysts: criticality/first power, NRC licensing, more hyperscaler deals. Risk: no commercial revenue until ~2027-28; licensing/fuel/construction execution; extreme valuation and volatility.

thesis.summary: First NRC-certified US SMR design positioned for the data-center power buildout.
Micro-cap US tungsten developer (historic IMA mine) with EXIM support into a supply crunch.
